places.name: Temple of the Tooth Relic (Sri Dalada Maligawa)
places.description: Sri Lanka's most sacred Buddhist temple, housing the relic of the tooth of Buddha. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.

places.name: Kandy Lake (Kiri Muhuda)
places.description: Artificial lake built by the last king of Kandy. Perfect for morning walks and photography.

places.name: Royal Botanical Gardens, Peradeniya
places.description: One of the finest botanical gardens in Asia, covering 147 acres with over 4,000 plant species including orchids, giant Javan fig trees, and spice gardens.

places.name: Kandy Cultural Centre & Dance Show
places.description: Traditional Kandyan dance performance featuring fire-walking and cultural heritage shows.

places.name: Udawatta Kele Sanctuary
places.description: A protected rainforest reserve in the heart of Kandy. Home to monkeys, birds, and tropical flora.

activities: Tea tasting at a local tea house
Shopping at Kandy City Centre Mall
Sunset walk along Bahirawakanda Temple hill

places.name: Nine Arch Bridge (Demodara Bridge)
places.description: A stunning colonial-era viaduct bridge built entirely of stone and brick with no steel. Best viewed when the train passes through lush green jungle.

places.name: Little Adam's Peak
places.description: An easy hike offering panoramic views of Ella Rock, the valley, and tea plantations. Ideal for sunrise.

places.name: Ella Rock
places.description: A challenging hike through tea estates and jungle leading to a stunning 360° view of the Ella valley.

places.name: Ravana Falls
places.description: A 25-metre cascade waterfall associated with the legend of King Ravana. A popular spot for swimming.

places.name: Diyaluma Falls
places.description: Sri Lanka's second tallest waterfall at 220 meters with natural rock pools at the top for swimming.

activities: Train ride from Ella to Kandy (scenic route)
Tea plantation tour and factory visit
White water rafting on Kelani River

places.name: Galle Fort
places.description: A 17th-century Dutch fort, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Contains bastions, ramparts, colonial buildings, boutique hotels, cafes, and art galleries.
places.best_time: Late afternoon for sunset walk on ramparts

places.name: Unawatuna Beach
places.description: A crescent-shaped bay with calm waters ideal for swimming and snorkeling, with coral reef and sea turtles.

places.name: Jungle Beach (Rumassala)
places.description: A secluded beach accessible through a jungle trail. Crystal clear water and excellent snorkeling.

places.name: Galle Lighthouse
places.description: Sri Lanka's oldest working lighthouse, built in 1938. Located inside the Galle Fort walls with sea views.

places.name: Sea Turtle Hatchery (Kosgoda)
places.description: A conservation project protecting endangered sea turtles. Visitors can watch hatchlings and learn about conservation.

activities: Whale watching boat tour (November to April)
Snorkeling and diving in coral reef
Shopping handcraft souvenirs inside the Fort
Cooking class — traditional Sri Lankan seafood curry

places.name: Gangaramaya Temple
places.description: A culturally rich Buddhist temple containing a mix of Sri Lankan, Thai, Indian, and Chinese architectural styles.

places.name: Pettah Market
places.description: Colombo's oldest and busiest bazaar, famous for spices, electronics, and local street food.

places.name: Galle Face Green
places.description: A large urban park along the seafront. Famous for street food, kite flying, and Colombo sunset views.

places.name: Colombo National Museum
places.description: The premier museum of Sri Lanka, housing royal regalia, ancient artifacts, and natural history exhibitions.

activities: Shopping at One Galle Face Mall
